title: TENANT-WIZARD-DEEP
scope: Phase 47 [WIZARD-MIGRATE] shipped TenantOnboardingService + CaretakerOnboardingService as acknowledgement-only 3-step scaffolds: tenant step 2 (KYC) and step 3 (payment method) just record a checkbox; caretaker step 2 (building assignment) just counts buildings; caretaker step 3 (notification preferences) only captures channel-level toggles (email/sms/whatsapp/push). The wizard is functional but shallow — a tenant completes the wizard with zero KYC documents submitted, a caretaker completes it without explicit per-building accept/decline. Phase 48 deepens both flows to real functionality. KYC: scout confirmed Phase 13 TenantKycSubmission + KycRequirement infrastructure is complete (requirements cascade building→landlord→global, document upload + review flow functional). Gap: User::hasCompletedKyc() returns bool only — no progress count for the wizard to surface. Payment method: scout confirmed NO TenantPaymentMethod model exists today; StripeCustomer is SaaS-billing-only, Payment.payment_method is per-payment string. Phase 48 ships tenant_payment_methods table + model + service for stored M-Pesa/bank credentials. Caretaker assignment: scout confirmed buildings.caretaker_id is single-FK only, no audit trail of when/who/why assigned. Phase 48 adds caretaker_assignments append-only audit table (pending/accepted/declined). Notification preferences: scout confirmed NotificationPreference has 13 per-type boolean columns already; Phase 47 only captures 5 channel toggles. Phase 48 surfaces per-type granularity in step 3. Vue polish: TenantSteps + CaretakerSteps scaffolds are visually plain — progress bar, validation error feedback, branded styling all queued.

categories.TENANT-KYC-BRIDGE: User::kycProgress() accessor returns {required, completed, pending, rejected, percent} with Cache::remember(5min) for wizard surfacing; TenantOnboardingService::processKyc reads it + advance() gates on having submitted all required (pending review acceptable, no hard block); runbook subsection.
categories.TENANT-PAYMENT-METHOD: tenant_payment_methods table (id, user_id, type enum [mpesa|bank|card], details_encrypted JSON encrypted via Crypt, is_default, verified_at, soft-deletes for DPA-3 retention); TenantPaymentMethod model + hasMany on User; TenantPaymentMethodService::store + setDefault + softDelete; TenantOnboardingService::processPaymentMethod creates a real TenantPaymentMethod row from form data instead of just acknowledging.
categories.CARETAKER-ASSIGNMENT-UX: caretaker_assignments append-only audit table (id, caretaker_id, building_id, status enum [pending|accepted|declined], assigned_at, decided_at, decision_reason, soft-deletes) — buildings.caretaker_id stays as the canonical assignment, this table captures the workflow + audit; CaretakerAssignmentService::recordAssignment (called from existing Invitation accept flow), ::accept + ::decline; CaretakerOnboardingService::processBuildingAssignmentAck flips pending assignments to accepted (default) or declined based on form input.
categories.CARETAKER-NOTIF-PREFS: CaretakerOnboardingService::processNotificationPreferences writes per-type columns (caretaker_invitation_enabled, maintenance_notice_enabled, general_enabled + ticket-related) in addition to channel columns from Phase 47; validateCaretakerStep step 3 rules extended to accept the new per-type fields; NotificationPreference::caretakerTypes static helper returns the relevant subset for UI rendering.
categories.WIZARD-PROGRESS-UX: Pages/Onboarding/Components/WizardProgressBar.vue shared between TenantSteps + CaretakerSteps (reads currentStep + totalSteps from props); form-error display with form.errors + processing state on submit button; branded styling matching the landlord Index.vue gradient + ring-shadow palette (Phase 31 visual language).
categories.CI: Phase48TenantWizardDeepSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog asserting all 5 categories' invariants hold; docs/runbooks/onboarding.md gains a Phase-48 [TENANT-WIZARD-DEEP] section; docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gains a sev4 row for tenant_kyc_blocked_count (visibility — tenants who complete the wizard but still have pending KYC reviews).

findings.title: User::kycProgress() accessor returns wizard-ready progress shape
findings.evidence: User::hasCompletedKyc() returns bool only. The wizard step 2 needs progress info (3 of 5 docs uploaded, 2 pending review) to surface a real checklist. Recomputing on every step view is expensive (3+ queries against tenant_kyc_submissions joined to kyc_requirements); a 5-min Cache::remember is correct for wizard scope (KYC submissions don't change minute-to-minute during onboarding).
findings.remediation: App\Models\User::kycProgress(): array — returns ['required' => N, 'submitted' => N, 'approved' => N, 'pending' => N, 'rejected' => N, 'percent' => 0..100, 'remaining_labels' => ['proof_of_income', ...]]. Wraps in Cache::remember("user:{$id}:kyc-progress", 300, ...). Cache key invalidates via TenantKycSubmission::saved listener (Phase-46 pattern). Phase48 watchdog asserts the shape + caching behaviour.

findings.title: TenantOnboardingService::processKyc reads kycProgress + gates advance on submitted (not approved)
findings.evidence: Phase 47's processKyc is a pure no-op acknowledgement — the wizard treats the step as done regardless of KYC state. That means a tenant can complete onboarding without uploading anything. Phase 48 gates: advance proceeds when all required docs have been submitted (pending or approved); a tenant doesn't have to wait for landlord review to finish onboarding, but does have to submit. If 'submitted < required' the writer returns false + the controller's withErrors path surfaces a 'Please complete your KYC documents at /complete-profile' message.
findings.remediation: TenantOnboardingService::processKyc($data, $user) reads $progress = $user->kycProgress(); if ($progress['submitted'] < $progress['required']) return false; else return true. The acknowledgement checkbox in TenantSteps step 2 stays but is now informational only — the actual gate is the count. lang/{en,sw,ar}/onboarding.php gain tenant.kyc.incomplete error key surfaced via withErrors.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/onboarding.md gains 'Tenant KYC step deepening' subsection
findings.evidence: Operator-facing docs need to reflect the new gate — a tenant who completes onboarding wizard but has 0 KYC submitted is a sign of someone bypassing the wizard via direct URL manipulation. The runbook should explain how to spot + fix.
findings.remediation: Append a 'Tenant KYC step deepening (Phase 48)' subsection under the Phase-47 [WIZARD-MIGRATE] section: kycProgress shape, the submitted-vs-required gate, how to inspect a tenant's progress (php artisan tinker; User::find(N)->kycProgress()), and how Cache key invalidation works via TenantKycSubmission saved listener.

findings.title: tenant_payment_methods table + TenantPaymentMethod model + User::tenantPaymentMethods hasMany
findings.evidence: Scout confirmed there is NO persistent tenant payment-method storage today. StripeCustomer is SaaS-billing-only (landlord subscriptions). Payment.payment_method is a per-payment string with no recurring/saved semantics. A tenant who wants their landlord to auto-debit rent has nowhere to put their M-Pesa number or bank details. The Phase 47 step 3 acknowledgement is therefore informational only; Phase 48 needs the persistent layer behind it.
findings.remediation: Migration `create_tenant_payment_methods_table`: id, user_id (FK), type ENUM [mpesa,bank,card] NOT NULL, details_encrypted JSON (stores TYPE-SPECIFIC details — mpesa: {phone}, bank: {bank_name, account_number, account_name}, card: {brand, last4, stripe_payment_method_id}), is_default BOOL, verified_at TIMESTAMP NULL, soft-deletes (DPA-3 retention pattern). Unique index on (user_id, type) — a user has at most one method per type. App\Models\TenantPaymentMethod with $casts['details_encrypted'] => 'encrypted:json' (Laravel Crypt-backed JSON cast). User::tenantPaymentMethods(): HasMany.

findings.title: App\Services\Tenant\TenantPaymentMethodService::store + setDefault + softDelete
findings.evidence: Encryption-at-rest of payment details (account numbers, phone numbers used for M-Pesa) is the DPA-1 + BREACH-PROTECTION-2 invariant. Direct model fillable + ->update() risks bypassing the encrypted cast if someone fills details_encrypted as a string. A small service enforces the canonical write path.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Tenant\TenantPaymentMethodService::store(User $user, string $type, array $details, bool $isDefault = false): TenantPaymentMethod. setDefault() unsets is_default on the user's other methods of the same type before flipping (single-default-per-type invariant). softDelete() preserves the row but flips deleted_at for the DPA-3 retention window. All writes go through the service; the watchdog source-greps for any $model->details_encrypted = direct-set callsites (should be zero outside the service).

findings.title: TenantOnboardingService::processPaymentMethod creates a real TenantPaymentMethod row from form data
findings.evidence: Phase 47 step 3 is a no-op acknowledgement (just returns true). Phase 48 step 3 collects type + details + is_default from the form and calls TenantPaymentMethodService::store(). If the user submits no details (the acknowledgement-only path stays for users without a default method ready), the step still advances — no required gate. The point is: when they DO provide details, they get persisted.
findings.remediation: TenantOnboardingService::processPaymentMethod($data, $user): if !empty($data['type']) AND !empty($data['details']) then call $service->store($user, $data['type'], $data['details'], $data['is_default'] ?? false). Validate via OnboardingController::validateTenantStep step 3: type in [mpesa,bank,card], details required if type present, type-specific sub-rules (mpesa phone regex, bank account_number length). Phase48 test creates a tenant, submits step 3 with mpesa + 0712345678, asserts a TenantPaymentMethod row exists with encrypted details_encrypted.

findings.title: caretaker_assignments append-only audit table
findings.evidence: buildings.caretaker_id is single-FK + landlord-driven via Invitation accept flow. There's no audit of WHEN it was assigned, whether the caretaker accepted, or whether they have multiple buildings to acknowledge. A caretaker reaching step 2 should see a list of pending assignments + an accept/decline action per building. The legacy buildings.caretaker_id stays as the canonical 'currently assigned' link; caretaker_assignments captures the workflow history.
findings.remediation: Migration `create_caretaker_assignments_table`: id, caretaker_id (FK users), building_id (FK buildings), status ENUM [pending,accepted,declined], assigned_at TIMESTAMP, decided_at TIMESTAMP NULL, decision_reason VARCHAR NULL, soft-deletes. Unique index on (caretaker_id, building_id) — one assignment row per (caretaker, building) pair. App\Models\CaretakerAssignment + relationships Building::caretakerAssignments + User::caretakerAssignments. Phase48 test asserts table + model + cascade-on-building-delete behavior.

findings.title: App\Services\Caretaker\CaretakerAssignmentService::recordAssignment + ::accept + ::decline
findings.evidence: The existing Invitation accept flow sets users.role = 'caretaker' + users.landlord_id but does NOT write buildings.caretaker_id (per scout). Phase 48 introduces an explicit assignment service: recordAssignment() called from the same Invitation accept listener (or from a new landlord-facing endpoint) writes a pending CaretakerAssignment row + sets buildings.caretaker_id (idempotent via firstOrCreate); accept() flips status → accepted + decided_at = now; decline() flips status → declined + clears buildings.caretaker_id (so landlord knows they need to re-assign).
findings.remediation: App\Services\Caretaker\CaretakerAssignmentService with three methods. recordAssignment($caretaker, $building): firstOrCreate on (caretaker_id, building_id) — assigned_at = now, status = 'pending'. accept($assignment): DB::transaction flips status + bumps decided_at + leaves buildings.caretaker_id as-is. decline($assignment, $reason = null): DB::transaction flips status + bumps decided_at + sets buildings.caretaker_id = null on the linked building (so landlord can re-assign). Listener on InvitationAccepted dispatches recordAssignment if invitation.role === 'caretaker' AND invitation.property_id → resolve buildings under that property.

findings.title: CaretakerOnboardingService::processBuildingAssignmentAck reads + writes accept/decline
findings.evidence: Phase 47's processBuildingAssignmentAck only counts assigned buildings. Phase 48 reads pending CaretakerAssignment rows for the caretaker AND flips them based on form input. The form sends {accept: [building_id, ...], decline: [building_id, ...]}; the service walks each and calls the appropriate CaretakerAssignmentService method. After processing, return false if the user accepted zero AND has pending rows (gate to retry); return true otherwise.
findings.remediation: CaretakerOnboardingService::processBuildingAssignmentAck($data, $user): $pending = CaretakerAssignment::where('caretaker_id', $user->id)->pending()->get(); if ($pending->isEmpty()) return true (no-op). For $id in $data['accept']: $service->accept($assignment). For $id in $data['decline']: $service->decline($assignment, $data['decline_reason'][$id] ?? null). Return true if every pending row was decided. validateCaretakerStep step 2 accepts accept[] + decline[] + decline_reason[] arrays.

findings.title: CaretakerOnboardingService::processNotificationPreferences writes per-type columns in addition to channels
findings.evidence: Phase 47's processNotificationPreferences captures only channel toggles (email/sms/whatsapp/push). NotificationPreference has 13 per-type boolean columns from Phase 28 — caretakers see way more than they need from rent-reminder/invoice notifications (those are tenant-facing). The caretaker-relevant subset is maintenance_notice_enabled + general_enabled + caretaker_invitation_enabled + tenant_invitation_enabled (the workflow-driven notifications). Phase 48 writes both channels AND types.
findings.remediation: CaretakerOnboardingService::processNotificationPreferences($data, $user) extends to write maintenance_notice_enabled / general_enabled / caretaker_invitation_enabled / tenant_invitation_enabled / lease_expiry_enabled in addition to email/sms/whatsapp/push. NotificationPreference::withoutGlobalScopes()->updateOrCreate same pattern. Phase48 test creates a caretaker, submits step 3 with maintenance_notice_enabled=true + sms_enabled=false, asserts the saved row has the right shape.

findings.title: OnboardingController::validateCaretakerStep step 3 rules extended to accept per-type fields
findings.evidence: Today validateCaretakerStep step 3 has only channel rules (email_enabled, sms_enabled, etc., all nullable|boolean). Phase 48 adds per-type rule list. Without explicit allow-listing, a hostile caretaker could submit unrelated booleans (eviction_notice_enabled — which they have no business toggling) and pollute the NotificationPreference row.
findings.remediation: validateCaretakerStep step 3 rules grow to include each caretaker-relevant per-type column with nullable|boolean. Rules are still pass-only — falsy values write false. The CaretakerOnboardingService array_filter ensures null fields are dropped on the writer side (back-compat behavior preserved).

findings.title: App\Models\NotificationPreference::caretakerTypes() static helper
findings.evidence: The CaretakerSteps.vue Vue component needs to render which per-type toggles to show. Hardcoding the list in Vue means future schema changes (add a new column, deprecate another) get out of sync. A model-side static method is the canonical source.
findings.remediation: NotificationPreference::caretakerTypes(): array — returns ['maintenance_notice_enabled', 'general_enabled', 'caretaker_invitation_enabled', 'tenant_invitation_enabled', 'lease_expiry_enabled']. CaretakerSteps.vue receives this list as a prop from getCaretakerStepProps (a new method in OnboardingController) and renders a checkbox per item with $t('notification.types.{type}') labels.

findings.title: resources/js/Pages/Onboarding/Components/WizardProgressBar.vue shared between TenantSteps + CaretakerSteps
findings.evidence: Phase 47 scaffolds have no progress indicator — a user on step 2 of 3 doesn't see they're 67% through. The landlord Index.vue has a gradient progress bar component (lines 280-294 in scout output). Phase 48 extracts it into a shared component with currentStep + totalSteps props and reuses across the role scaffolds.
findings.remediation: resources/js/Pages/Onboarding/Components/WizardProgressBar.vue: <script setup>defineProps({currentStep, totalSteps}); const pct = computed(() => Math.round(currentStep / totalSteps * 100))</script><template>... gradient div with width: pct% ...</template>. TenantSteps + CaretakerSteps mount it at top: <WizardProgressBar :currentStep :totalSteps='3' />.

findings.title: TenantSteps + CaretakerSteps surface form errors + submit-disabled state
findings.evidence: Phase 47 scaffolds have submit buttons but no error display + the :disabled='form.processing' guard is the only UX feedback. If TenantOnboardingService::processKyc returns false (KYC incomplete gate), the user gets a back-redirect with a flash error but the Vue component doesn't render it. Inertia exposes form.errors as a reactive object — Phase 48 wires it in.
findings.remediation:
Both scaffolds gain
{{ form.errors.error }}
below the submit button. Also a global
for back-redirect flash errors. Submit button shows a spinner icon when form.processing.

findings.title: Branded styling — gradient bg + ring shadows matching landlord Index.vue palette
findings.evidence: Phase 47 scaffolds use plain gray-50 + white cards. Brand consistency means matching the Phase 31 visual language: bg-gradient-to-br from-indigo-50 via-white to-purple-50 + rounded-2xl + shadow-sm with ring-1 ring-gray-100. Cheap cosmetic change with high perceived-polish ROI.
findings.remediation: TenantSteps + CaretakerSteps wrap their root <div> with the same gradient palette. Card divs gain rounded-2xl shadow-sm ring-1 ring-gray-100. Buttons match bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-700 (already present).

findings.title: Phase48TenantWizardDeepSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog
findings.evidence: Phase 48 changes span 6 files + 2 migrations + 2 new services + 2 new models + 2 Vue components — drift is easy without a single watchdog. The Phase 47/46/45 pattern (one *SurfaceTest per cycle) consolidates the invariants in one source-grep + integration suite.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/Onboarding/Phase48TenantWizardDeepSurfaceTest with 12+ tests: (a) User::kycProgress() returns the right shape with Cache::remember, (b) processKyc gates on submitted >= required, (c) tenant_payment_methods table exists with encrypted JSON cast, (d) TenantPaymentMethodService::store + setDefault + softDelete behave, (e) caretaker_assignments table exists with status enum, (f) CaretakerAssignmentService::accept + ::decline behave, (g) processBuildingAssignmentAck reads + writes correctly, (h) processNotificationPreferences writes per-type cols, (i) validateCaretakerStep step 3 includes per-type rules, (j) NotificationPreference::caretakerTypes returns the expected list, (k) WizardProgressBar component file exists + is imported in both scaffolds, (l) runbook + alert-thresholds rows present.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/onboarding.md Phase-48 TENANT-WIZARD-DEEP section
findings.evidence: Operator docs need: how kycProgress works, how to debug a stuck tenant at step 2, how the caretaker_assignments table relates to buildings.caretaker_id, how to wipe a tenant_payment_methods row for DPA-3 right-to-be-forgotten requests, how the per-type notification toggles interact with the existing Phase 28 surface.
findings.remediation: Append a '# Phase-48 TENANT-WIZARD-DEEP extensions (2026-05-17)' section under the Phase-47 section. Subsections: 'Tenant KYC step deepening', 'Tenant payment-method storage', 'Caretaker assignment workflow', 'Caretaker per-type notification preferences', 'Visual UX polish'. Each subsection has 3-5 lines of operator-facing guidance + a troubleshooting bullet.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gains tenant_kyc_blocked_count sev4 row
findings.evidence: Tenants who reach step 2 + can't advance because KYC is incomplete are a growth-funnel signal — if the count spikes, the landlord's KYC requirement set is too strict or the tenant flow is confusing. Sev4 (not sev3) because it's not an outage; it's a UX KPI worth visibility.
findings.remediation: alert-thresholds.md gains a row: tenant_kyc_blocked_count sev4 threshold 20/24h, gauge emitted by an existing or new daily cron. For Phase 48 the gauge wiring is optional — the alert row + threshold definition is the documented commitment. If the Phase 48 cycle has spare scope, the gauge ships; otherwise it carries as a sub-scope deferral.
